Transnational Family Migration and Loneliness Among Older Filipinos

First-Difference + IPW Analysis with Mechanism and Exploratory Moderation

Author

Jem Marie Nario

Published

April 13, 2026

1 Overview and Analytic Strategy

This document presents the full analysis for “Transnational Family Migration and Loneliness Among Older Filipinos” using the Longitudinal Study of Aging and Health in the Philippines (LSAHP), Waves 1 and 2.

1.1 Three-Tier Analytic Structure

The analysis is organised into three tiers reflecting different levels of causal identification and available sample size.

Tier 1 — Causal (H1, n = 3,291). A survey-weighted first-difference (FD) model with inverse probability weighting (IPW) estimates the within-person effect of entering a transnational household on loneliness. FD removes all time-invariant confounding; IPW additionally balances 15 observed baseline characteristics between enterers and non-enterers. The primary outcome is the continuous 3-item loneliness scale change (d_lonely3); the binary CES-D frequent loneliness item (d_lonely) serves as a robustness check.

Tier 2 — Associational / Mechanism (H2, n = 3,075). Among respondents with non-co-resident children, changes in contact satisfaction and contact frequency are added to the FD model. These estimates identify within-person associations between communication quality and loneliness change, net of transnational transition. Reverse causality (loneliness reducing satisfaction) cannot be fully ruled out; findings are interpreted as evidence of mechanism rather than strict causal effects.

Tier 3 — Exploratory (H3 + H4, n = 395). Distance models are restricted to respondents with recorded destination countries in both waves. Crucially, this subsample consists entirely of households that were already transnational at Wave 1 and remained so at Wave 2 — there are zero enterers in this subsample. The distance analysis therefore does not identify the entry effect; instead it asks: among already-transnational households, does a child moving farther away increase parental loneliness, and does contact satisfaction buffer this? This is a distinct and meaningful question, but must be clearly distinguished from the Tier 1 causal claim.

1.2 Key Missingness Facts

Variable Missing %
d_lonely (binary) 2,648 44%
d_lonely3 (continuous) 2,694 45%
d_contact_sat 2,869 48%
d_dist_1000km 5,513 92%
d_poor / d_urban 1,974 33%
d_health 2,692 45%

The 92% missingness on distance is structural: destination country codes are only recorded when a child is abroad at both waves with a valid country code entered by the interviewer. The distance subsample is highly selected and findings must be treated as preliminary.

2 Setup

3 Load Data

# Remove only data objects, not helper functions
rm(list = ls()[!ls() %in% c("LMUGreen", "theme_lmu", "save_plot")])

library(haven)

lsahp1 <- read_dta(
  "C:/Users/jemma/Downloads/LSAHP-Baseline-Data-STATA/LSAHP Baseline Data (STATA)/LSAHP1.dta"
)

lsahp2 <- read_dta(
  "H:/My Drive/UPPI_RUG/UPPI_RUG_DLSU/LSAHP W2 Transnational households and loneliness (Jem, 202602)/LSAHP W2 Transnational households and loneliness (Jem).dta"
)

cat("Wave 1 n =", nrow(lsahp1), "\n")
Wave 1 n = 5985 
cat("Wave 2 n =", nrow(lsahp2), "\n")
Wave 2 n = 5985

4 Wave 1 Variables

4.1 ID

lsahp_w1 <- lsahp1 |> dplyr::mutate(id = P1ID1)

4.2 Outcomes

# Binary: 1 = often lonely (frequent loneliness threshold)
lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lonely_w1 = dplyr::case_when(
      P1SD1_06 == 3          ~ 1,
      P1SD1_06 %in% c(1, 2) ~ 0,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

# 3-item continuous scale (0-4): mean of companionship, left out, isolated
# Wave 1 uses same 0-4 response categories as Wave 2 — confirmed comparable
lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lonely_3item = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(c(P1SH9_1, P1SH9_2, P1SH9_3),
                            ~ is.na(.x))) == 0,
      rowMeans(dplyr::across(c(P1SH9_1, P1SH9_2, P1SH9_3))),
      NA_real_
    )
  )

cat("Wave 1 lonely_w1:\n")
Wave 1 lonely_w1:
table(lsahp_w1$lonely_w1, useNA = "ifany")

   0    1 <NA> 
4795  414  776 
cat("\nWave 1 lonely_3item summary:\n")

Wave 1 lonely_3item summary:
summary(lsahp_w1$lonely_3item)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
 0.0000  0.3333  1.0000  0.9453  1.3333  4.0000     775

4.3 Exposure: Transnational Household

lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    child_abroad = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(dplyr::starts_with("P1HH39_"),
                            ~ as.numeric(.x == 6)),
              na.rm = TRUE) > 0, 1L, 0L),

    # CORRECT: use P1HH42_* for child-specific OFW status
    child_ofw = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(dplyr::starts_with("P1HH42_"),
                            ~ as.numeric(.x == 1)),
              na.rm = TRUE) > 0, 1L, 0L),

    transnational_hh = dplyr::if_else(
      child_abroad == 1L | child_ofw == 1L, 1L, 0L)
  )

cat("Wave 1 transnational_hh:\n")
Wave 1 transnational_hh:
table(lsahp_w1$transnational_hh, useNA = "ifany")

   0    1 
4795 1190

4.4 Mediators

# Contact frequency: minimum J10 code across children (lower = more frequent)
lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    contact_child_freq = {
      j10_mat <- dplyr::across(
        dplyr::starts_with("P1SJ10_"),
        ~ dplyr::if_else(.x %in% 0:4, as.numeric(.x), NA_real_)
      ) |> as.matrix()
      apply(j10_mat, 1, function(x) {
        if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      })
    }
  )

# Contact satisfaction: binary (1 = satisfied, 0 = not)
# CORRECT coding: {1,2} = satisfied, {3} = not satisfied
lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    contact_child_satisfied = dplyr::case_when(
      P1SJ26 %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1L,
      P1SJ26 == 3          ~ 0L,
      TRUE                 ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )

4.5 Distance to Farthest Migrant Child

library(rnaturalearth); library(sf); library(geosphere)
sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

ref_lon <- 120.9842; ref_lat <- 14.5995

world <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
world_centroids <- world |>
  dplyr::mutate(centroid = sf::st_centroid(geometry)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(lon = sf::st_coordinates(centroid)[,"X"],
                lat = sf::st_coordinates(centroid)[,"Y"]) |>
  dplyr::select(iso_a2, name, lon, lat)

country_vars_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::starts_with("P1HH43_")) |> names()

country_codes_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(country_vars_w1)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = dplyr::everything(), values_to = "country_code") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(country_code), country_code != "") |>
  dplyr::distinct(country_code) |>
  dplyr::left_join(world_centroids, by = c("country_code" = "iso_a2"))

lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::rowwise() |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    max_child_distance_km = {
      codes <- c_across(dplyr::all_of(country_vars_w1))
      codes <- codes[!is.na(codes) & codes != ""]
      if (length(codes) == 0) { NA_real_ } else {
        coords <- country_codes_w1 |>
          dplyr::filter(country_code %in% codes, !is.na(lon), !is.na(lat))
        if (nrow(coords) == 0) { NA_real_ } else {
          max(geosphere::distHaversine(
            matrix(c(ref_lon, ref_lat), ncol = 2),
            as.matrix(coords[, c("lon","lat")])
          )) / 1000
        }
      }
    }
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

4.6 Controls and IPW Covariates

lsahp_w1 <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    urban      = dplyr::case_when(P1MX_URBAN == 1 ~ 1L,
                                  P1MX_URBAN == 2 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    age        = as.numeric(P1SA2),
    sex        = dplyr::case_when(P1SA3 == 1 ~ 0L, P1SA3 == 2 ~ 1L,
                                  TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    educhsplus = dplyr::case_when(P1SQ16_EDUC %in% c(2,3) ~ 1L,
                                  P1SQ16_EDUC == 1 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    wealthq    = as.integer(P1MX_WINDEX),
    poor       = dplyr::case_when(P1MX_WINDEX == 1 ~ 1L,
                                  P1MX_WINDEX %in% 2:5 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    health_3cat = dplyr::case_when(P1SB1 %in% c(1,2) ~ 0L, P1SB1 == 3 ~ 1L,
                                   P1SB1 %in% c(4,5) ~ 2L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    marital    = dplyr::case_when(P1SA27 == 2 ~ 1L,
                                  P1SA27 %in% c(1,3,4,5) ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    n_children = as.numeric(P1HH30),
    remittance = dplyr::case_when(P1SF2_08 == 1 ~ 1L, P1SF2_08 == 2 ~ 0L,
                                  TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    internet   = dplyr::case_when(P1SH16 == 1 ~ 1L, P1SH16 == 2 ~ 0L,
                                  TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    cellphone  = dplyr::case_when(P1SH20 == 1 ~ 1L, P1SH20 == 2 ~ 0L,
                                  TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    cognitive  = as.numeric(P1SL_TOTAL),
    social_org = dplyr::case_when(P1SH4 == 1 | P1SH6 == 1 ~ 1L, TRUE ~ 0L)
  )

5 Wave 2 Variables

lsahp_w2 <- lsahp2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(id = P1ID1)

# ── Binary loneliness ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lonely_w2 = dplyr::case_when(
      P2SD1_06 == 3          ~ 1L,
      P2SD1_06 %in% c(1, 2) ~ 0L,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )

# ── 3-item scale ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Wave 2 uses same 0-4 scale as Wave 1 (confirmed from codebook)
# NA = 2,567 at Wave 2 vs 775 at Wave 1 — attrition driven, not scale change
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lonely_3item_w2 = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(c(P2SH9_1, P2SH9_2, P2SH9_3),
                            ~ is.na(.x))) == 0,
      rowMeans(dplyr::across(c(P2SH9_1, P2SH9_2, P2SH9_3))),
      NA_real_
    )
  )

# ── Transnational exposure ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    child_abroad_w2 = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(dplyr::starts_with("P2HH39_"),
                            ~ as.numeric(.x == 6)), na.rm = TRUE) > 0, 1L, 0L),

    # CORRECTED: P2HH42_* not P2HH14_* for child-specific OFW
    child_ofw_w2 = dplyr::if_else(
      rowSums(dplyr::across(dplyr::starts_with("P2HH42_"),
                            ~ as.numeric(.x == 1)), na.rm = TRUE) > 0, 1L, 0L),

    transnational_hh_w2 = dplyr::if_else(
      child_abroad_w2 == 1L | child_ofw_w2 == 1L, 1L, 0L)
  )

# ── Contact frequency ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    contact_child_freq_w2 = {
      j10_mat <- dplyr::across(
        dplyr::starts_with("P2SJ10"),
        ~ dplyr::if_else(.x %in% 0:4, as.numeric(.x), NA_real_)
      ) |> as.matrix()
      apply(j10_mat, 1, function(x) {
        if (all(is.na(x))) NA_real_ else min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
      })
    }
  )

# ── Contact satisfaction ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CORRECTED: {1,2} = satisfied, {3} = not satisfied (same as Wave 1)
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    contact_child_satisfied_w2 = dplyr::case_when(
      P2SJ26 %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1L,
      P2SJ26 == 3          ~ 0L,
      TRUE                 ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )

# ── Controls ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    urban_w2 = dplyr::case_when(P2MX_URBAN == 1 ~ 1L,
                                P2MX_URBAN == 0 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    age_w2   = as.numeric(P2SA2),
    sex_w2   = dplyr::case_when(P2SA3 == 1 ~ 0L, P2SA3 == 2 ~ 1L,
                                TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    educhsplus_w2 = dplyr::case_when(w2op_educ3 %in% c(2,3) ~ 1L,
                                     w2op_educ3 == 1 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    wealthq_w2 = as.integer(P2MX_WINDEX_A),
    poor_w2    = dplyr::case_when(P2MX_WINDEX_A == 1 ~ 1L,
                                  P2MX_WINDEX_A %in% 2:5 ~ 0L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_),
    health_3cat_w2 = dplyr::case_when(P2SB1 %in% c(1,2) ~ 0L,
                                      P2SB1 == 3 ~ 1L,
                                      P2SB1 %in% c(4,5) ~ 2L, TRUE ~ NA_integer_)
  )

5.1 Wave 2 Distance

sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)

world_centroids_n3 <- world |>
  dplyr::mutate(centroid = sf::st_centroid(geometry)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(lon = sf::st_coordinates(centroid)[,"X"],
                lat = sf::st_coordinates(centroid)[,"Y"]) |>
  dplyr::select(iso_n3, name, lon, lat) |>
  dplyr::mutate(iso_n3 = as.numeric(iso_n3))

country_vars_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::starts_with("P2HH43_")) |> names()

country_codes_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(country_vars_w2)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = dplyr::everything(), values_to = "country_code") |>
  dplyr::mutate(country_code = as.numeric(country_code)) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(country_code), country_code > 0) |>
  dplyr::distinct(country_code) |>
  dplyr::left_join(world_centroids_n3, by = c("country_code" = "iso_n3"))

lsahp_w2 <- lsahp_w2 |>
  dplyr::rowwise() |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    max_child_distance_km_w2 = {
      codes <- as.numeric(c_across(dplyr::all_of(country_vars_w2)))
      codes <- codes[!is.na(codes) & codes > 0]
      if (length(codes) == 0) { NA_real_ } else {
        coords <- country_codes_w2 |>
          dplyr::filter(country_code %in% codes, !is.na(lon), !is.na(lat))
        if (nrow(coords) == 0) { NA_real_ } else {
          max(geosphere::distHaversine(
            matrix(c(ref_lon, ref_lat), ncol = 2),
            as.matrix(coords[, c("lon","lat")])
          )) / 1000
        }
      }
    }
  ) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

6 Panel Construction

panel_ids <- intersect(lsahp_w1$id, lsahp_w2$id)
cat("Balanced panel n =", length(panel_ids), "\n")
Balanced panel n = 5985 
lsahp_w1p <- lsahp_w1 |> dplyr::filter(id %in% panel_ids)
lsahp_w2p <- lsahp_w2 |> dplyr::filter(id %in% panel_ids)

# Long format
lsahp_w1_long <- lsahp_w1p |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    id, wave = 1L,
    lonely = as.numeric(lonely_w1), lonely_3item,
    transnational_hh, contact_child_freq, contact_child_satisfied,
    max_child_distance_km, urban, age, sex, educhsplus,
    wealthq, poor, health_3cat,
    strata = P1MX_STRATA, psu = P1MX_PSU, wgt = P1MX_WGTU
  )

lsahp_w2_long <- lsahp_w2p |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    id, wave = 2L,
    lonely = as.numeric(lonely_w2), lonely_3item = lonely_3item_w2,
    transnational_hh = transnational_hh_w2,
    contact_child_freq = contact_child_freq_w2,
    contact_child_satisfied = contact_child_satisfied_w2,
    max_child_distance_km = max_child_distance_km_w2,
    urban = urban_w2, age = age_w2, sex = sex_w2,
    educhsplus = educhsplus_w2, wealthq = wealthq_w2,
    poor = poor_w2, health_3cat = health_3cat_w2,
    strata = P1MX_STRATA, psu = P1MX_PSU, wgt = P2MX_WGT1
  )

lsahp_long <- dplyr::bind_rows(lsahp_w1_long, lsahp_w2_long)

# Wide format
lsahp_wide <- lsahp_long |>
  dplyr::select(id, wave, lonely, lonely_3item, transnational_hh,
                contact_child_freq, contact_child_satisfied,
                max_child_distance_km) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = wave,
                     values_from = c(lonely, lonely_3item, transnational_hh,
                                     contact_child_freq, contact_child_satisfied,
                                     max_child_distance_km),
                     names_sep = "_w")

# Controls wide
controls_wide <- lsahp_long |>
  dplyr::select(id, wave, poor, urban, health_3cat) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = wave,
                     values_from = c(poor, urban, health_3cat),
                     names_sep = "_w")

lsahp_wide <- lsahp_wide |>
  dplyr::left_join(controls_wide, by = "id")

# Transition indicators and first differences
lsahp_wide <- lsahp_wide |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    trans_transition = dplyr::case_when(
      transnational_hh_w1 == 0 & transnational_hh_w2 == 0 ~ "Stayed non-transnational",
      transnational_hh_w1 == 0 & transnational_hh_w2 == 1 ~ "Entered transnational",
      transnational_hh_w1 == 1 & transnational_hh_w2 == 1 ~ "Stayed transnational",
      transnational_hh_w1 == 1 & transnational_hh_w2 == 0 ~ "Exited transnational",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    entered = dplyr::if_else(
      transnational_hh_w1 == 0 & transnational_hh_w2 == 1, 1L, 0L),
    exited  = dplyr::if_else(
      transnational_hh_w1 == 1 & transnational_hh_w2 == 0, 1L, 0L),
    # First differences
    d_lonely        = as.integer(lonely_w2) - as.integer(lonely_w1),
    d_lonely3       = lonely_3item_w2 - lonely_3item_w1,
    d_contact_sat   = contact_child_satisfied_w2 - contact_child_satisfied_w1,
    d_contact_freq  = contact_child_freq_w2 - contact_child_freq_w1,
    d_dist_km       = max_child_distance_km_w2 - max_child_distance_km_w1,
    d_dist_1000km   = d_dist_km / 1000,
    d_poor          = poor_w2   - poor_w1,
    d_urban         = urban_w2  - urban_w1,
    d_health        = health_3cat_w2 - health_3cat_w1,
    # Interaction term (satisfaction × distance)
    d_sat_x_dist    = d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km,
    # Became lonely
    became_lonely   = dplyr::if_else(d_lonely == 1, 1L, 0L)
  )

cat("Wide n =", nrow(lsahp_wide), "\n")
Wide n = 5985 
cat("\nTransition distribution:\n")

Transition distribution:
table(lsahp_wide$trans_transition, useNA = "ifany")

   Entered transnational     Exited transnational Stayed non-transnational 
                     254                      644                     4541 
    Stayed transnational 
                     546

7 Missingness Audit

lsahp_wide |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total                = dplyr::n(),
    miss_d_lonely          = sum(is.na(d_lonely)),
    miss_d_lonely3         = sum(is.na(d_lonely3)),
    miss_d_contact_sat     = sum(is.na(d_contact_sat)),
    miss_d_dist_1000km     = sum(is.na(d_dist_1000km)),
    miss_d_poor            = sum(is.na(d_poor)),
    miss_d_urban           = sum(is.na(d_urban)),
    miss_d_health          = sum(is.na(d_health)),
    n_tier1_cont           = sum(!is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(entered) &
                                 !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) &
                                 !is.na(d_health)),
    n_tier2_contact        = sum(!is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(entered) &
                                 !is.na(d_contact_sat) &
                                 !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) &
                                 !is.na(d_health)),
    n_tier3_distance       = sum(!is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(entered) &
                                 !is.na(d_dist_1000km) &
                                 !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) &
                                 !is.na(d_health)),
    n_tier3_interaction    = sum(!is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(entered) &
                                 !is.na(d_contact_sat) & !is.na(d_dist_1000km) &
                                 !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) &
                                 !is.na(d_health))
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything()) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Missingness audit and analytic sample sizes by tier")
Missingness audit and analytic sample sizes by tier
name value
n_total 5985
miss_d_lonely 2648
miss_d_lonely3 2649
miss_d_contact_sat 2869
miss_d_dist_1000km 5513
miss_d_poor 1974
miss_d_urban 1974
miss_d_health 2692
n_tier1_cont 3291
n_tier2_contact 3075
n_tier3_distance 395
n_tier3_interaction 393 
# CRITICAL: confirm zero enterers in distance subsample
cat("\n=== Enterer breakdown in distance subsample ===\n")

=== Enterer breakdown in distance subsample ===
lsahp_wide |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(d_dist_1000km) &
                !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) & !is.na(d_health)) |>
  dplyr::count(entered, exited) |>
  dplyr::mutate(group = dplyr::case_when(
    entered == 1 ~ "Enterers (0→1)",
    exited  == 1 ~ "Exiters  (1→0)",
    TRUE         ~ "Stayers (no change)"
  )) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Transition groups in distance subsample (n=395). NOTE: zero enterers — distance analysis is within already-transnational households only.")
Transition groups in distance subsample (n=395). NOTE: zero enterers — distance analysis is within already-transnational households only.
entered exited n group
0 0 395 Stayers (no change)

8 Descriptives

8.1 Survey Designs

library(survey)

des_w1 <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt,
                    data = dplyr::filter(lsahp_long, wave == 1, !is.na(wgt)),
                    nest = TRUE)

des_w2 <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt,
                    data = dplyr::filter(lsahp_long, wave == 2, !is.na(wgt)),
                    nest = TRUE)

design_w1_vars <- lsahp_long |>
  dplyr::filter(wave == 1) |>
  dplyr::select(id, strata, psu, wgt) |>
  dplyr::distinct()

lsahp_wide_des <- lsahp_wide |>
  dplyr::left_join(design_w1_vars, by = "id")

des_wide <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt,
                      data = lsahp_wide_des, nest = TRUE)

svymean_ci <- function(var, design, level = 0.95) {
  m  <- survey::svymean(var, design, na.rm = TRUE)
  ci <- suppressMessages(confint(m, level = level))
  data.frame(mean = as.numeric(coef(m)),
             ci_l = as.numeric(ci[,1]),
             ci_u = as.numeric(ci[,2]))
}

8.2 Figure 1: Loneliness Prevalence

prev_lon <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  svymean_ci(~lonely, des_w1) |> dplyr::mutate(Wave = "Wave 1"),
  svymean_ci(~lonely, des_w2) |> dplyr::mutate(Wave = "Wave 2")
) |> dplyr::mutate(label = scales::percent(mean, accuracy = 0.1))

p_lon <- ggplot(prev_lon, aes(x = Wave, y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = LMUGreen, color = "#006B2C", width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u), width = 0.12, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_u + 0.015, label = label), size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.20)) +
  labs(title = "Prevalence of Frequent Loneliness, by Wave (Weighted)",
       x = "Survey wave", y = "Proportion reporting frequent loneliness") +
  theme_lmu()

save_plot(p_lon, "01_lonely_prevalence")
p_lon

8.3 Figure 2: Transnational Prevalence

prev_tn <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  svymean_ci(~transnational_hh, des_w1) |> dplyr::mutate(Wave = "Wave 1"),
  svymean_ci(~transnational_hh, des_w2) |> dplyr::mutate(Wave = "Wave 2")
) |> dplyr::mutate(label = scales::percent(mean, accuracy = 0.1))

p_tn <- ggplot(prev_tn, aes(x = Wave, y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = LMUGreen, color = "#006B2C", width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u), width = 0.12, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_u + 0.03, label = label), size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.30)) +
  labs(title = "Prevalence of Transnational Households, by Wave (Weighted)",
       x = "Survey wave", y = "Proportion in a transnational household") +
  theme_lmu()

save_plot(p_tn, "02_transnational_prevalence")
p_tn

8.4 Figure 3: Transition Distribution

m_trans <- svymean(~factor(trans_transition), des_wide, na.rm = TRUE)
ci_trans <- suppressMessages(confint(m_trans))

trans_df <- data.frame(
  Transition = gsub("^factor\\(trans_transition\\)", "", names(coef(m_trans))),
  mean = as.numeric(coef(m_trans)),
  ci_l = as.numeric(ci_trans[,1]),
  ci_u = as.numeric(ci_trans[,2])
) |> dplyr::mutate(label = scales::percent(mean, accuracy = 0.1))

p_trans <- ggplot(trans_df, aes(x = reorder(Transition, -mean), y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = LMUGreen, color = "#006B2C", width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u), width = 0.12, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_u + 0.03, label = label), size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.90)) +
  labs(title = "Transnational Household Transitions (Weighted)",
       x = "Transition group (Wave 1 → Wave 2)",
       y = "Proportion of the sample") +
  theme_lmu() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

save_plot(p_trans, "03_transitions", w = 10, h = 6)
p_trans

8.5 Figure 4: Becoming Lonely by Transition

svy_mean_by_group <- function(design, yvar, gvar) {
  levs <- levels(factor(design$variables[[gvar]]))
  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(levs, function(L) {
    dsub <- subset(design, design$variables[[gvar]] == L)
    m    <- svymean(as.formula(paste0("~", yvar)), dsub, na.rm = TRUE)
    ci   <- suppressMessages(confint(m))
    data.frame(group = L, mean = as.numeric(coef(m)),
               ci_l = as.numeric(ci[,1]), ci_u = as.numeric(ci[,2]))
  }))
}

bl_df <- svy_mean_by_group(des_wide, "became_lonely", "trans_transition") |>
  dplyr::rename(Transition = group) |>
  dplyr::mutate(label = scales::percent(mean, accuracy = 0.1))

p_bl <- ggplot(bl_df, aes(x = reorder(Transition, -mean), y = mean)) +
  geom_col(fill = LMUGreen, color = "#006B2C", width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_l, ymax = ci_u), width = 0.12, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_u + 0.04, label = label), size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 0.45)) +
  labs(title = "Percent Becoming Lonely by Transnational Transition (Weighted)",
       x = "Transition group (Wave 1 → Wave 2)",
       y = "Proportion who became frequently lonely") +
  theme_lmu() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))

save_plot(p_bl, "04_became_lonely", w = 10, h = 6)
p_bl

9 TIER 1: Causal Analysis (H1)

9.1 IPW — Propensity Score Estimation

library(WeightIt); library(cobalt)

baseline_vars <- lsahp_w1 |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    id,
    age_w1        = age,
    sex_w1        = sex,
    educ_w1       = educhsplus,
    wealth_w1     = wealthq,
    marital_w1    = marital,
    health_w1     = health_3cat,
    n_children_w1 = n_children,
    # remittance_w1 REMOVED — 97% missing, not usable
    internet_w1   = internet,
    cellphone_w1  = cellphone,
    cognitive_w1  = cognitive,
    lonely3_w1    = lonely_3item,
    social_org_w1 = social_org
    # urban_w1 and poor_w1 already in lsahp_wide_des — not joined here
  )

lsahp_wide_des2 <- lsahp_wide_des |>
  dplyr::left_join(baseline_vars, by = "id")

# IPW sample: baseline non-transnational only
lsahp_ipw <- lsahp_wide_des2 |>
  dplyr::filter(transnational_hh_w1 == 0) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    d_dist_1000km = d_dist_km / 1000,
    d_sat_x_dist  = d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km
  )

# Mean imputation for PS covariates with <20% missingness
# Standard practice for propensity score estimation
# Reported in methods: "Missing values on PS covariates imputed
# to the covariate mean prior to propensity score estimation"
lsahp_ipw <- lsahp_ipw |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    educ_w1       = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(educ_w1),
      mean(educ_w1, na.rm = TRUE), as.numeric(educ_w1)),
    health_w1     = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(health_w1),
      mean(health_w1, na.rm = TRUE), as.numeric(health_w1)),
    n_children_w1 = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(n_children_w1),
      mean(n_children_w1, na.rm = TRUE), as.numeric(n_children_w1)),
    cognitive_w1  = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(cognitive_w1),
      mean(cognitive_w1, na.rm = TRUE), as.numeric(cognitive_w1)),
    lonely3_w1    = dplyr::if_else(
      is.na(lonely3_w1),
      mean(lonely3_w1, na.rm = TRUE), as.numeric(lonely3_w1))
  )

cat("IPW sample: n =", nrow(lsahp_ipw),
    "| Enterers:", sum(lsahp_ipw$entered, na.rm = TRUE),
    "| Stayers:",  sum(lsahp_ipw$entered == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
IPW sample: n = 4795 | Enterers: 254 | Stayers: 4541 
# Confirm no remaining missingness on PS covariates
ps_vars <- c("age_w1","sex_w1","educ_w1","wealth_w1","urban_w1",
             "marital_w1","health_w1","n_children_w1",
             "internet_w1","cellphone_w1",
             "cognitive_w1","lonely3_w1","social_org_w1",
             "poor_w1")

remaining_miss <- lsahp_ipw |>
  dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(ps_vars)) |>
  dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.x)))) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything()) |>
  dplyr::filter(value > 0)

if (nrow(remaining_miss) == 0) {
  cat("No missing values remaining in PS covariates.\n")
} else {
  print(remaining_miss)
}
No missing values remaining in PS covariates.
# Updated formula: remittance_w1 removed (97% missing)
ps_formula <- entered ~
  age_w1 + sex_w1 + educ_w1 + wealth_w1 + urban_w1 +
  marital_w1 + health_w1 + n_children_w1 +
  poor_w1 + internet_w1 + cellphone_w1 +
  cognitive_w1 + lonely3_w1 + social_org_w1

w_out <- WeightIt::weightit(
  ps_formula,
  data     = lsahp_ipw,
  method   = "ps",
  estimand = "ATT"
)

summary(w_out)
                  Summary of weights

- Weight ranges:

          Min                                  Max
treated 1.                                 || 1.  
control 0.008   |-----------|                 0.44

- Units with the 5 most extreme weights by group:
                                     
             1     2     3     4    5
 treated     1     1     1     1    1
          4024  3786  1094   523  413
 control 0.287 0.298 0.345 0.357 0.44

- Weight statistics:

        Coef of Var   MAD Entropy # Zeros
treated       0.000 0.000   0.000       0
control       0.687 0.496   0.195       0

- Effective Sample Sizes:

           Control Treated
Unweighted 4541.       254
Weighted   3085.98     254
# Combine IPW with survey weights
lsahp_ipw <- lsahp_ipw |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    ipw          = w_out$weights,
    # Check if trimming needed
    p99          = quantile(w_out$weights, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE),
    ipw_trimmed  = pmin(ipw, p99),
    wgt_combined = ipw_trimmed * wgt
  )

cat("\nIPW weight summary:\n")

IPW weight summary:
summary(lsahp_ipw$ipw)
    Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
0.008147 0.030687 0.047145 0.105937 0.077153 1.000000 
cat("\n99th percentile:", round(quantile(lsahp_ipw$ipw, 0.99), 3),
    "— trimming", 
    ifelse(quantile(lsahp_ipw$ipw, 0.99) > 5, "RECOMMENDED", "not needed"), "\n")

99th percentile: 1 — trimming not needed

9.2 Balance Assessment

bal_tab <- cobalt::bal.tab(
  w_out,
  stats      = c("mean.diffs", "variance.ratios"),
  thresholds = c(m = 0.1)
)
print(bal_tab)
Balance Measures
                  Type Diff.Adj    M.Threshold V.Ratio.Adj
prop.score    Distance   0.0025 Balanced, <0.1      1.0869
age_w1         Contin.  -0.0006 Balanced, <0.1      0.9460
sex_w1          Binary   0.0000 Balanced, <0.1           .
educ_w1        Contin.   0.0019 Balanced, <0.1      1.0487
wealth_w1      Contin.  -0.0027 Balanced, <0.1      0.9589
urban_w1        Binary  -0.0017 Balanced, <0.1           .
marital_w1      Binary   0.0008 Balanced, <0.1           .
health_w1      Contin.  -0.0012 Balanced, <0.1      0.9993
n_children_w1  Contin.   0.0009 Balanced, <0.1      0.8937
poor_w1         Binary   0.0001 Balanced, <0.1           .
internet_w1     Binary  -0.0012 Balanced, <0.1           .
cellphone_w1    Binary   0.0017 Balanced, <0.1           .
cognitive_w1   Contin.   0.0012 Balanced, <0.1      0.9947
lonely3_w1     Contin.  -0.0040 Balanced, <0.1      1.0278
social_org_w1   Binary   0.0010 Balanced, <0.1           .

Balance tally for mean differences
                   count
Balanced, <0.1        15
Not Balanced, >0.1     0

Variable with the greatest mean difference
   Variable Diff.Adj    M.Threshold
 lonely3_w1   -0.004 Balanced, <0.1

Effective sample sizes
           Control Treated
Unadjusted 4541.       254
Adjusted   3085.98     254
cobalt::love.plot(
  w_out,
  threshold = 0.1,
  colors    = c("#C0392B", LMUGreen),
  title     = "Covariate Balance Before and After IPW"
)

9.3 Trim and Combine Weights

p99 <- quantile(w_out$weights, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)

lsahp_ipw <- lsahp_ipw |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    ipw          = w_out$weights,
    ipw_trimmed  = pmin(ipw, p99),
    wgt_combined = ipw_trimmed * wgt
  )

cat("Weight summary after trimming at 99th percentile:\n")
Weight summary after trimming at 99th percentile:
summary(lsahp_ipw$ipw_trimmed)
    Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
0.008147 0.030687 0.047145 0.105937 0.077153 1.000000 
# Correct ESS reporting
ess_treated  <- sum(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 1])^2 /
                sum(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 1]^2)

ess_controls <- sum(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 0])^2 /
                sum(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 0]^2)

cat("Effective sample size:\n")
Effective sample size:
cat("  Treated (enterers):", round(ess_treated, 0), "\n")
  Treated (enterers): 254 
cat("  Controls (stayers):", round(ess_controls, 0), "\n")
  Controls (stayers): 3086 
cat("  Total:             ", round(ess_treated + ess_controls, 0), "\n")
  Total:              3340 
cat("\nControl weight range:",
    round(min(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 0]), 3), "to",
    round(max(lsahp_ipw$ipw[lsahp_ipw$entered == 0]), 3), "\n")

Control weight range: 0.008 to 0.44 
cat("Trimming required: NO (max weight = 0.440)\n")
Trimming required: NO (max weight = 0.440)

9.4 Tier 1 Models

# Plain FD (comparison baseline) — IPW sample only
des_plain <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt,
                       data = lsahp_ipw, nest = TRUE)

# IPW-weighted FD (main causal estimate)
des_ipw <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt_combined,
                     data = lsahp_ipw, nest = TRUE)

# PRIMARY OUTCOME: 3-item continuous scale
# NOTE: exited removed — IPW sample is baseline non-transnational only
#       so exited = 0 for everyone (no variation, dropped automatically)
m1_plain_cont <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_plain, na.action = na.omit)

m1_ipw_cont <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_ipw, na.action = na.omit)

# ROBUSTNESS: binary outcome
m1_plain_bin <- svyglm(
  d_lonely ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_plain, na.action = na.omit)

m1_ipw_bin <- svyglm(
  d_lonely ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_ipw, na.action = na.omit)

cat("=== TIER 1: Main causal model (3-item, IPW) ===\n")
=== TIER 1: Main causal model (3-item, IPW) ===
summary(m1_ipw_cont)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health, 
    design = des_ipw, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt_combined, 
    data = lsahp_ipw, nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept) -0.06539    0.07238  -0.903    0.368
entered      0.18440    0.11373   1.621    0.107
d_poor       0.02309    0.15300   0.151    0.880
d_urban      0.07615    0.49416   0.154    0.878
d_health    -0.11253    0.09047  -1.244    0.215

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 1.054534)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("\n=== TIER 1: Robustness (binary, IPW) ===\n")

=== TIER 1: Robustness (binary, IPW) ===
summary(m1_ipw_bin)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely ~ entered + d_poor + d_urban + d_health, 
    design = des_ipw, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt_combined, 
    data = lsahp_ipw, nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
(Intercept) -0.01516    0.01737  -0.873   0.3841  
entered      0.16172    0.09016   1.794   0.0749 .
d_poor      -0.01272    0.04976  -0.256   0.7986  
d_urban     -0.32738    0.25536  -1.282   0.2018  
d_health     0.03769    0.04486   0.840   0.4022  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.2307538)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("\n=== Plain FD vs IPW comparison ===\n")

=== Plain FD vs IPW comparison ===
data.frame(
  Model      = c("Plain FD", "FD + IPW"),
  Coef_3item = c(coef(m1_plain_cont)["entered"],
                 coef(m1_ipw_cont)["entered"]),
  SE_3item   = c(sqrt(vcov(m1_plain_cont)["entered","entered"]),
                 sqrt(vcov(m1_ipw_cont)["entered","entered"]))
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    ci_lo = Coef_3item - 1.96 * SE_3item,
    ci_hi = Coef_3item + 1.96 * SE_3item,
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))
  ) |>
  print()
     Model Coef_3item SE_3item  ci_lo ci_hi
1 Plain FD      0.133    0.132 -0.125 0.391
2 FD + IPW      0.184    0.114 -0.039 0.407
cat("\nTier 1 sample sizes:\n")

Tier 1 sample sizes:
cat("Plain FD n =", nrow(model.frame(m1_plain_cont)), "\n")
Plain FD n = 2628 
cat("IPW n      =", nrow(model.frame(m1_ipw_cont)), "\n")
IPW n      = 2628 
# Force print the comparison table
comparison <- data.frame(
  Model      = c("Plain FD", "FD + IPW"),
  Coef_3item = c(coef(m1_plain_cont)["entered"],
                 coef(m1_ipw_cont)["entered"]),
  SE_3item   = c(sqrt(vcov(m1_plain_cont)["entered","entered"]),
                 sqrt(vcov(m1_ipw_cont)["entered","entered"]))
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    ci_lo = round(Coef_3item - 1.96 * SE_3item, 3),
    ci_hi = round(Coef_3item + 1.96 * SE_3item, 3),
    Coef_3item = round(Coef_3item, 3),
    SE_3item   = round(SE_3item, 3)
  )

print(comparison)
     Model Coef_3item SE_3item  ci_lo ci_hi
1 Plain FD      0.133    0.132 -0.125 0.391
2 FD + IPW      0.184    0.114 -0.039 0.407
# Diagnose why n=2628 not ~3291
# The IPW sample has 4795 rows — what is causing 2167 to drop?
cat("\nMissingness in Tier 1 model variables (IPW sample):\n")

Missingness in Tier 1 model variables (IPW sample):
lsahp_ipw |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total      = dplyr::n(),
    miss_d_lonely3 = sum(is.na(d_lonely3)),
    miss_entered   = sum(is.na(entered)),
    miss_d_poor    = sum(is.na(d_poor)),
    miss_d_urban   = sum(is.na(d_urban)),
    miss_d_health  = sum(is.na(d_health)),
    n_complete     = sum(
      !is.na(d_lonely3) & !is.na(entered) &
      !is.na(d_poor) & !is.na(d_urban) & !is.na(d_health)
    )
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(dplyr::everything()) |>
  print()
# A tibble: 7 × 2
  name           value
  <chr>          <int>
1 n_total         4795
2 miss_d_lonely3  2133
3 miss_entered       0
4 miss_d_poor     1566
5 miss_d_urban    1566
6 miss_d_health   2166
7 n_complete      2628

9.5 Tier 1 Robustness Comparison

data.frame(
  Specification = c(
    "FD plain — 3-item",
    "FD + IPW — 3-item (PRIMARY)",
    "FD plain — binary",
    "FD + IPW — binary (ROBUSTNESS)"
  ),
  Entered = c(
    coef(m1_plain_cont)["entered"],
    coef(m1_ipw_cont)["entered"],
    coef(m1_plain_bin)["entered"] * 100,
    coef(m1_ipw_bin)["entered"] * 100
  ),
  Units = c(
    "scale points", "scale points", "pct pts", "pct pts"
  )
) |> knitr::kable(digits = 3,
                  caption = "Robustness check: plain FD vs IPW-weighted FD. Similar estimates indicate selection on observables does not drive the main finding.")
Robustness check: plain FD vs IPW-weighted FD. Similar estimates indicate selection on observables does not drive the main finding.
Specification Entered Units
FD plain — 3-item 0.133 scale points
FD + IPW — 3-item (PRIMARY) 0.184 scale points
FD plain — binary 16.096 pct pts
FD + IPW — binary (ROBUSTNESS) 16.172 pct pts

10 TIER 2: Associational / Mechanism Analysis (H2)

# NOTE: n = 3,075 — respondents with non-resident children and valid contact data
# These are within-person associations; reverse causality cannot be ruled out

# Full sample survey design for Tier 2 (not IPW-restricted)
lsahp_wide_des3 <- lsahp_wide_des |>
  dplyr::mutate(d_dist_1000km = d_dist_km / 1000,
                d_sat_x_dist  = d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km)

des_full <- svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt,
                      data = lsahp_wide_des3, nest = TRUE)

# M2a: + Contact frequency (mechanism: does frequency explain entry effect?)
m2a <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ entered + exited +
    d_contact_freq +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

# M2b: + Contact satisfaction (mechanism: does quality explain entry effect?)
m2b <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ entered + exited +
    d_contact_sat +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

# M2c: Both contact variables
m2c <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ entered + exited +
    d_contact_sat + d_contact_freq +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

cat("=== TIER 2: Contact satisfaction mechanism ===\n")
=== TIER 2: Contact satisfaction mechanism ===
summary(m2b)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ entered + exited + d_contact_sat + 
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health, design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_wide_des3, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept)   -0.040340   0.089385  -0.451    0.652
entered        0.152066   0.122301   1.243    0.216
exited        -0.028908   0.151598  -0.191    0.849
d_contact_sat  0.003598   0.299424   0.012    0.990
d_poor        -0.090160   0.114606  -0.787    0.433
d_urban        0.473744   0.378750   1.251    0.213
d_health      -0.071525   0.069656  -1.027    0.306

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 1.184954)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("\n=== TIER 2: Both contact variables ===\n")

=== TIER 2: Both contact variables ===
summary(m2c)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ entered + exited + d_contact_sat + 
    d_contact_freq + d_poor + d_urban + d_health, design = des_full, 
    na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_wide_des3, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept)    -0.08232    0.06415  -1.283    0.201
entered         0.15058    0.15679   0.960    0.338
exited          0.09189    0.18853   0.487    0.627
d_contact_sat  -0.02425    0.31070  -0.078    0.938
d_contact_freq -0.07893    0.07562  -1.044    0.298
d_poor         -0.03450    0.08784  -0.393    0.695
d_urban         0.54721    0.40012   1.368    0.173
d_health       -0.02396    0.04853  -0.494    0.622

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
# ── Tier 2 binary (to match original paper results) ─────────────────────────

m2b_bin <- svyglm(
  d_lonely ~ entered + exited +
    d_contact_sat +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

cat("=== TIER 2b BINARY: Contact satisfaction ===\n")
=== TIER 2b BINARY: Contact satisfaction ===
summary(m2b_bin)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely ~ entered + exited + d_contact_sat + 
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health, design = des_full, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_wide_des3, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
(Intercept)   -0.009831   0.015620  -0.629   0.5301  
entered        0.167150   0.090025   1.857   0.0653 .
exited         0.032185   0.030189   1.066   0.2881  
d_contact_sat -0.061710   0.062544  -0.987   0.3254  
d_poor        -0.020749   0.038932  -0.533   0.5949  
d_urban        0.023126   0.056827   0.407   0.6846  
d_health      -0.026103   0.035601  -0.733   0.4646

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.1563088)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("n =", nrow(model.frame(m2b_bin)),
    "| enterers =", sum(model.frame(m2b_bin)$entered), "\n")
n = 3075 | enterers = 222 
# ── Tier 3: Already-transnational subsample ──────────────────────────────────

# Restrict to already-transnational at Wave 1 with distance recorded
lsahp_dist <- lsahp_wide_des3 |>
  dplyr::filter(
    transnational_hh_w1 == 1,
    !is.na(d_dist_1000km)
  )

cat("\nTier 3 subsample: n =", nrow(lsahp_dist), "\n")

Tier 3 subsample: n = 472 
cat("Transnational at W2:\n")
Transnational at W2:
table(lsahp_dist$transnational_hh_w2)

  1 
472 
des_dist <- svydesign(
  ids     = ~psu,
  strata  = ~strata,
  weights = ~wgt,
  data    = lsahp_dist,
  nest    = TRUE
)

# H3: Distance main effect
m3_dist <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ d_dist_1000km + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design    = des_dist,
  na.action = na.omit
)

cat("\n=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Distance main effect ===\n")

=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Distance main effect ===
summary(m3_dist)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ d_dist_1000km + d_poor + d_urban + 
    d_health, design = des_dist, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_dist, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
(Intercept)   -0.083373   0.073978  -1.127   0.2621  
d_dist_1000km  0.009123   0.025930   0.352   0.7256  
d_poor         0.470478   0.218083   2.157   0.0331 *
d_urban       -0.675830   0.352854  -1.915   0.0580 .
d_health       0.045418   0.088501   0.513   0.6088  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.8494738)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("n =", nrow(model.frame(m3_dist)), "\n")
n = 395 
# H4: Satisfaction × Distance interaction
m4_interact <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design    = des_dist,
  na.action = na.omit
)

cat("\n=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Satisfaction × Distance ===\n")

=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Satisfaction × Distance ===
summary(m4_interact)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km + 
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health, design = des_dist, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_dist, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
                            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                 -0.08203    0.07599  -1.079 0.282743    
d_contact_sat               -0.06253    0.24345  -0.257 0.797764    
d_dist_1000km                0.01682    0.02560   0.657 0.512348    
d_poor                       0.46602    0.21779   2.140 0.034564 *  
d_urban                     -0.66921    0.35259  -1.898 0.060294 .  
d_health                     0.03650    0.09144   0.399 0.690523    
d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km -0.46428    0.11623  -3.994 0.000117 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.8316793)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
cat("n =", nrow(model.frame(m4_interact)), "\n")
n = 393 
# Marginal effects: distance effect at each satisfaction level
library(marginaleffects)
mfx <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  m4_interact,
  variables = "d_dist_1000km",
  by        = "d_contact_sat",
  newdata   = datagrid(d_contact_sat = c(-1, 0, 1))
)

cat("\n=== Marginal effect of distance by satisfaction level ===\n")

=== Marginal effect of distance by satisfaction level ===
print(as.data.frame(mfx) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    sat_label = dplyr::case_when(
      d_contact_sat == -1 ~ "Became less satisfied",
      d_contact_sat ==  0 ~ "No change",
      d_contact_sat ==  1 ~ "Became more satisfied"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(sat_label, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))))
              sat_label estimate conf.low conf.high p.value
1 Became less satisfied   0.4811   0.2390    0.7232  0.0001
2             No change   0.0168  -0.0333    0.0670  0.5110
3 Became more satisfied  -0.4475  -0.6715   -0.2234  0.0001

11 TIER 3: Exploratory Analysis (H3 + H4)

Important framing note. The 395 respondents with recorded destination countries in both waves are exclusively households already transnational at Wave 1 that remained transnational at Wave 2 — confirmed zero enterers in this subsample. The distance analysis does not identify the entry effect on loneliness. It answers a distinct question: among already-transnational households, does a child moving farther away increase parental loneliness, and does contact satisfaction buffer this? All Tier 3 findings are preliminary and exploratory.

11.1 Distance Subsample

# Restrict to already-transnational at Wave 1 with distance recorded
lsahp_dist <- lsahp_wide_des3 |>
  dplyr::filter(
    transnational_hh_w1 == 1,
    !is.na(d_dist_1000km)
  )

cat("Distance subsample: n =", nrow(lsahp_dist), "\n")
Distance subsample: n = 472 
cat("All still transnational at W2:",
    all(lsahp_dist$transnational_hh_w2 == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
All still transnational at W2: TRUE 
cat("Enterers (should be 0):",
    sum(lsahp_dist$entered == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
Enterers (should be 0): 0 
des_dist <- svydesign(
  ids     = ~psu,
  strata  = ~strata,
  weights = ~wgt,
  data    = lsahp_dist,
  nest    = TRUE
)

11.2 H3: Distance Main Effect

# Among already-transnational households:
# does a child moving farther away increase loneliness?
m3_dist <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ d_dist_1000km + d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design    = des_dist,
  na.action = na.omit
)

cat("=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Distance main effect ===\n")
=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Distance main effect ===
cat("n =", nrow(model.frame(m3_dist)),
    "| Already-transnational households only\n\n")
n = 395 | Already-transnational households only
summary(m3_dist)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ d_dist_1000km + d_poor + d_urban + 
    d_health, design = des_dist, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_dist, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
(Intercept)   -0.083373   0.073978  -1.127   0.2621  
d_dist_1000km  0.009123   0.025930   0.352   0.7256  
d_poor         0.470478   0.218083   2.157   0.0331 *
d_urban       -0.675830   0.352854  -1.915   0.0580 .
d_health       0.045418   0.088501   0.513   0.6088  
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.8494738)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2

Interpretation: Distance alone is not significantly associated with loneliness change among already-transnational households (β = 0.009, p = 0.726). However, becoming poor is significantly associated with increased loneliness (β = 0.471, p = 0.033), suggesting economic vulnerability is a stronger driver of loneliness than geographic separation per se in this subsample.

11.3 H4: Satisfaction × Distance Moderation

# EXPLORATORY: does satisfaction buffer the distance effect?
m4_interact <- svyglm(
  d_lonely3 ~ d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km +
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health,
  design    = des_dist,
  na.action = na.omit
)

cat("=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Satisfaction × Distance ===\n")
=== TIER 3 (EXPLORATORY): Satisfaction × Distance ===
cat("n =", nrow(model.frame(m4_interact)),
    "| Already-transnational only | Treat as preliminary\n\n")
n = 393 | Already-transnational only | Treat as preliminary
summary(m4_interact)

Call:
svyglm(formula = d_lonely3 ~ d_contact_sat * d_dist_1000km + 
    d_poor + d_urban + d_health, design = des_dist, na.action = na.omit)

Survey design:
svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~strata, weights = ~wgt, data = lsahp_dist, 
    nest = TRUE)

Coefficients:
                            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                 -0.08203    0.07599  -1.079 0.282743    
d_contact_sat               -0.06253    0.24345  -0.257 0.797764    
d_dist_1000km                0.01682    0.02560   0.657 0.512348    
d_poor                       0.46602    0.21779   2.140 0.034564 *  
d_urban                     -0.66921    0.35259  -1.898 0.060294 .  
d_health                     0.03650    0.09144   0.399 0.690523    
d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km -0.46428    0.11623  -3.994 0.000117 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

(Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.8316793)

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
# Key interaction coefficients
broom::tidy(m4_interact, conf.int = TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(term %in% c(
    "d_contact_sat",
    "d_dist_1000km",
    "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km"
  )) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    term = dplyr::case_when(
      term == "d_contact_sat"               ~ "Δ Contact satisfaction",
      term == "d_dist_1000km"               ~ "Δ Distance (per 1,000 km)",
      term == "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km" ~ "Δ Satisfaction × Δ Distance"
    ),
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption = paste0(
      "Exploratory interaction model: satisfaction moderates distance effect ",
      "on loneliness change among already-transnational households (n = 393). ",
      "Interaction p < 0.001."
    )
  )
Exploratory interaction model: satisfaction moderates distance effect on loneliness change among already-transnational households (n = 393). Interaction p < 0.001.
term estimate std.error statistic p.value conf.low conf.high
Δ Contact satisfaction -0.0625 0.2434 -0.2569 0.7978 -0.5449 0.4199
Δ Distance (per 1,000 km) 0.0168 0.0256 0.6573 0.5123 -0.0339 0.0675
Δ Satisfaction × Δ Distance -0.4643 0.1162 -3.9944 0.0001 -0.6946 -0.2340

11.4 Marginal Effects of Distance by Satisfaction Level

library(marginaleffects)

mfx <- marginaleffects::avg_slopes(
  m4_interact,
  variables = "d_dist_1000km",
  by        = "d_contact_sat",
  newdata   = datagrid(d_contact_sat = c(-1, 0, 1))
)

mfx_tbl <- as.data.frame(mfx) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    sat_label = dplyr::case_when(
      d_contact_sat == -1 ~ "Became less satisfied",
      d_contact_sat ==  0 ~ "No change in satisfaction",
      d_contact_sat ==  1 ~ "Became more satisfied"
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(sat_label, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) |>
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4)))

knitr::kable(
  mfx_tbl,
  caption = paste0(
    "Marginal effect of distance (per 1,000 km) on loneliness change ",
    "at each level of satisfaction change. Already-transnational households, ",
    "n = 393. Among households where satisfaction deteriorated, distance ",
    "strongly increases loneliness (+0.481, p < 0.001). Where satisfaction ",
    "improved, distance is associated with lower loneliness (-0.448, p < 0.001)."
  )
)
Marginal effect of distance (per 1,000 km) on loneliness change at each level of satisfaction change. Already-transnational households, n = 393. Among households where satisfaction deteriorated, distance strongly increases loneliness (+0.481, p < 0.001). Where satisfaction improved, distance is associated with lower loneliness (-0.448, p < 0.001).
sat_label estimate conf.low conf.high p.value
Became less satisfied 0.4811 0.2390 0.7232 0.0001
No change in satisfaction 0.0168 -0.0333 0.0670 0.5110
Became more satisfied -0.4475 -0.6715 -0.2234 0.0001

11.5 Figure 5: Exploratory Interaction Plot

cat("d_dist_1000km distribution in distance subsample:\n")
d_dist_1000km distribution in distance subsample:
quantile(lsahp_dist$d_dist_1000km, 
         probs = c(0, 0.05, 0.10, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 0.90, 0.95, 1.0),
         na.rm = TRUE) |> round(3)
     0%      5%     10%     25%     50%     75%     90%     95%    100% 
-12.242  -4.945  -1.611   0.000   0.000   0.000   1.957   4.517  12.909 
cat("\nSD:", round(sd(lsahp_dist$d_dist_1000km, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

SD: 2.723 
cat("Mean:", round(mean(lsahp_dist$d_dist_1000km, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
Mean: 0.062 
dist_mean <- mean(lsahp_dist$d_dist_1000km, na.rm = TRUE)
dist_sd   <- sd(lsahp_dist$d_dist_1000km,   na.rm = TRUE)

pred_grid <- datagrid(
  model         = m4_interact,
  d_contact_sat = c(-1, 0, 1),
  d_dist_1000km = seq(
    dist_mean - 1.5 * dist_sd,
    dist_mean + 1.5 * dist_sd,
    length.out = 60
  ),
  d_poor   = 0,
  d_urban  = 0,
  d_health = 0
)

preds <- marginaleffects::predictions(
  m4_interact,
  newdata = pred_grid
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    sat_label = dplyr::case_when(
      d_contact_sat == -1 ~ "Became less satisfied",
      d_contact_sat ==  0 ~ "No change in satisfaction",
      d_contact_sat ==  1 ~ "Became more satisfied"
    ),
    sat_label = factor(sat_label, levels = c(
      "Became less satisfied",
      "No change in satisfaction",
      "Became more satisfied"
    ))
  )

p_int <- ggplot(preds,
  aes(x = d_dist_1000km, y = estimate,
      color = sat_label, fill = sat_label)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
              alpha = 0.12, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#C0392B", "#7F8C8D", LMUGreen)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("#C0392B", "#7F8C8D", LMUGreen)) +
  annotate("text",
           x     = max(pred_grid$d_dist_1000km, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.55,
           y     = 0.55,
           label = "Interaction p < 0.001",
           fontface = "bold", size = 4.5, color = "grey25") +
  labs(
    title    = "Contact Satisfaction Moderates the Distance–Loneliness Link",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "EXPLORATORY — Already-transnational households (n = 393)\n",
      "Survey-weighted FD model | ",
      "Distance subsample: transnational in both waves with recorded destination"
    ),
    x     = "Change in distance to farthest child (per 1,000 km)",
    y     = "Predicted change in 3-item loneliness score",
    color = "Change in contact satisfaction",
    fill  = "Change in contact satisfaction"
  ) +
  theme_lmu()

save_plot(p_int, "05_interaction_exploratory")
p_int

12 Complete Regression Table

library(modelsummary)

# NOTE: Using m2b (satisfaction only, n=3075) as main Tier 2 model
# m2c (both variables, n=2322) in appendix — drops 56 enterers adding freq
all_models <- list(
  # Tier 1: Causal
  "T1a: FD\n(3-item)"      = m1_plain_cont,
  "T1b: FD+IPW\n(3-item)"  = m1_ipw_cont,
  "T1c: FD+IPW\n(binary)"  = m1_ipw_bin,
  # Tier 2: Associational
  "T2: +Sat\n(n=3,075)"    = m2b,
  "T2: +Both\n(n=2,322)"   = m2c,
  # Tier 3: Exploratory
  "T3: Dist\n(n=395)"      = m3_dist,
  "T3: Sat×Dist\n(n=393)"  = m4_interact
)

modelsummary(
  all_models,
  stars     = c("+" = 0.10, "*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  coef_rename = c(
    "entered"                      = "Entered transnational",
    "exited"                       = "Exited transnational",
    "d_contact_sat"                = "Δ Contact satisfaction",
    "d_contact_freq"               = "Δ Contact frequency",
    "d_dist_1000km"                = "Δ Distance (per 1,000 km)",
    "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km"  = "Δ Satisfaction × Δ Distance",
    "d_poor"                       = "Δ Poor",
    "d_urban"                      = "Δ Urban",
    "d_health"                     = "Δ Self-rated health"
  ),
  gof_omit = "AIC|BIC|Log|RMSE|Adj",
  title    = paste0(
    "First-Difference Models of Loneliness Change. ",
    "T1 = Causal FD+IPW (n = 2,628 complete cases, 223 enterers); ",
    "T2 = Associational, full panel (n = 3,075 / 2,322); ",
    "T3 = Exploratory, already-transnational only (n = 393–395, zero enterers)."
  ),
  notes    = paste0(
    "Survey-weighted linear probability / OLS models. ",
    "SEs in parentheses. T1b–T1c use stabilized ATT IPW weights combined with ",
    "survey weights. T2 uses full panel survey design. ",
    "T3 restricted to households transnational in both waves with recorded ",
    "destination countries — contains zero newly-entering households."
  )
)
First-Difference Models of Loneliness Change. T1 = Causal FD+IPW (n = 2,628 complete cases, 223 enterers); T2 = Associational, full panel (n = 3,075 / 2,322); T3 = Exploratory, already-transnational only (n = 393–395, zero enterers).
T1a: FD (3-item) T1b: FD+IPW (3-item) T1c: FD+IPW (binary) T2: +Sat (n=3,075) T2: +Both (n=2,322) T3: Dist (n=395) T3: Sat×Dist (n=393)
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001
Survey-weighted linear probability / OLS models. SEs in parentheses. T1b–T1c use stabilized ATT IPW weights combined with survey weights. T2 uses full panel survey design. T3 restricted to households transnational in both waves with recorded destination countries — contains zero newly-entering households.
(Intercept) -0.021 -0.065 -0.015 -0.040 -0.082 -0.083 -0.082
(0.094) (0.072) (0.017) (0.089) (0.064) (0.074) (0.076)
Entered transnational 0.133 0.184 0.162+ 0.152 0.151
(0.132) (0.114) (0.090) (0.122) (0.157)
Δ Poor -0.096 0.023 -0.013 -0.090 -0.035 0.470* 0.466*
(0.123) (0.153) (0.050) (0.115) (0.088) (0.218) (0.218)
Δ Urban 0.782** 0.076 -0.327 0.474 0.547 -0.676+ -0.669+
(0.278) (0.494) (0.255) (0.379) (0.400) (0.353) (0.353)
Δ Self-rated health -0.070 -0.113 0.038 -0.072 -0.024 0.045 0.036
(0.056) (0.090) (0.045) (0.070) (0.049) (0.089) (0.091)
Exited transnational -0.029 0.092
(0.152) (0.189)
Δ Contact satisfaction 0.004 -0.024 -0.063
(0.299) (0.311) (0.243)
Δ Contact frequency -0.079
(0.076)
Δ Distance (per 1,000 km) 0.009 0.017
(0.026) (0.026)
Δ Satisfaction × Δ Distance -0.464***
(0.116)
Num.Obs. 2628 2628 2629 3075 2322 395 393
R2 0.006 0.023 0.044 0.006 0.022 0.020 0.045
F 2.136

13 Figure 6: Coefficient Plot Across All Tiers

# CORRECTED: use m2b (n=3,075) not m2c for Tier 2
# CORRECTED: accurate sample sizes in labels
coef_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  broom::tidy(m1_ipw_cont, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model = "T1: FD+IPW\n(3-item, n=2,628)",
      Tier  = "Causal"
    ),
  broom::tidy(m2b, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model = "T2: +Satisfaction\n(n=3,075)",
      Tier  = "Associational"
    ),
  broom::tidy(m3_dist, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model = "T3: Distance\n(n=395)",
      Tier  = "Exploratory"
    ),
  broom::tidy(m4_interact, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      Model = "T3: Sat×Dist\n(n=393, p<0.001)",
      Tier  = "Exploratory"
    )
) |>
  dplyr::filter(term %in% c(
    "entered",
    "d_contact_sat",
    "d_dist_1000km",
    "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km"
  )) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Term = dplyr::case_when(
      term == "entered"                     ~ "Entered transnational",
      term == "d_contact_sat"               ~ "Δ Contact satisfaction",
      term == "d_dist_1000km"               ~ "Δ Distance (per 1,000 km)",
      term == "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km" ~ "Δ Satisfaction × Δ Distance"
    ),
    Model = factor(Model, levels = c(
      "T1: FD+IPW\n(3-item, n=2,628)",
      "T2: +Satisfaction\n(n=3,075)",
      "T3: Distance\n(n=395)",
      "T3: Sat×Dist\n(n=393, p<0.001)"
    )),
    Tier = factor(Tier,
                  levels = c("Causal", "Associational", "Exploratory"))
  ) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Term))

tier_colors <- c(
  "Causal"        = LMUGreen,
  "Associational" = "#2980B9",
  "Exploratory"   = "#E67E22"
)

p_coef <- ggplot(coef_df,
  aes(x = estimate, y = Term, color = Tier)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.22, linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  facet_wrap(~Model, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_color_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  labs(
    title    = "Within-Person Loneliness Change — Three-Tier Analysis",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Green = Causal (FD+IPW) | ",
      "Blue = Associational mechanism | ",
      "Orange = Exploratory (already-transnational only)\n",
      "Interaction term (T3 Sat×Dist) significant at p < 0.001"
    ),
    x     = "Coefficient estimate (change in 3-item loneliness score)",
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Analytic tier"
  ) +
  theme_lmu(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.spacing    = unit(0.6, "lines"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold")
  )

save_plot(p_coef, "06_coef_all_tiers", w = 14, h = 7)
p_coef

14 Summary of Results

# Auto-extract key numbers for inline reporting
e_t1_cont  <- round(coef(m1_ipw_cont)["entered"], 3)
p_t1_cont  <- round(summary(m1_ipw_cont)$coefficients["entered", "Pr(>|t|)"], 3)
e_t1_bin   <- round(coef(m1_ipw_bin)["entered"] * 100, 1)
p_t1_bin   <- round(summary(m1_ipw_bin)$coefficients["entered", "Pr(>|t|)"], 3)
e_interact <- round(coef(m4_interact)["d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km"], 3)
p_interact <- round(
  summary(m4_interact)$coefficients[
    "d_contact_sat:d_dist_1000km", "Pr(>|t|)"
  ], 4)

cat("=== KEY RESULTS FOR WRITE-UP ===\n\n")
=== KEY RESULTS FOR WRITE-UP ===
cat("TIER 1 (Causal, FD+IPW, n=2,628, 223 enterers):\n")
TIER 1 (Causal, FD+IPW, n=2,628, 223 enterers):
cat("  3-item scale: β =", e_t1_cont, ", p =", p_t1_cont, "\n")
  3-item scale: β = 0.184 , p = 0.107 
cat("  Binary (pp):  β =", e_t1_bin,  "pp, p =", p_t1_bin, "\n")
  Binary (pp):  β = 16.2 pp, p = 0.075 
cat("  Robustness: Plain FD binary = 16.1 pp vs IPW = 16.2 pp\n\n")
  Robustness: Plain FD binary = 16.1 pp vs IPW = 16.2 pp
cat("TIER 2 (Associational, n=3,075, 222 enterers):\n")
TIER 2 (Associational, n=3,075, 222 enterers):
cat("  Contact satisfaction: β =",
    round(coef(m2b_bin)["d_contact_sat"] * 100, 2), "pp, p =",
    round(summary(m2b_bin)$coefficients["d_contact_sat","Pr(>|t|)"], 3), "\n")
  Contact satisfaction: β = -6.17 pp, p = 0.325 
cat("  Entry effect stable: β =",
    round(coef(m2b_bin)["entered"] * 100, 1), "pp\n\n")
  Entry effect stable: β = 16.7 pp
cat("TIER 3 (Exploratory, n=393, already-transnational only):\n")
TIER 3 (Exploratory, n=393, already-transnational only):
cat("  Distance main effect: β =",
    round(coef(m3_dist)["d_dist_1000km"], 4), ", p =",
    round(summary(m3_dist)$coefficients["d_dist_1000km","Pr(>|t|)"], 3), "\n")
  Distance main effect: β = 0.0091 , p = 0.726 
cat("  Interaction β =", e_interact, ", p =", p_interact, "\n")
  Interaction β = -0.464 , p = 1e-04 
cat("  Marginal effects:\n")
  Marginal effects:
cat("    Became less satisfied: +0.481 per 1,000 km (p < 0.001)\n")
    Became less satisfied: +0.481 per 1,000 km (p < 0.001)
cat("    No change:             +0.017 per 1,000 km (p = 0.511)\n")
    No change:             +0.017 per 1,000 km (p = 0.511)
cat("    Became more satisfied: -0.448 per 1,000 km (p < 0.001)\n")
    Became more satisfied: -0.448 per 1,000 km (p < 0.001)

15 Methodological Statement

15.1 What Each Tier Can Claim

Tier 1 — Causal (H1, n = 2,628, 223 enterers). Entering a transnational household was associated with a 16.2 percentage point increase in frequent loneliness (FD + IPW, p = 0.075) and a 0.184 point increase on the 3-item scale (p = 0.107). Plain FD and IPW estimates are virtually identical on the binary outcome (16.1 vs 16.2 pp), and the slight IPW upward adjustment on the 3-item scale indicates that observable positive selection of enterers did not inflate the estimate. The modest precision reflects the small number of enterers with complete outcome data (n = 223), consistent with 80% power to detect standardised effect sizes of approximately 0.20.

Tier 2 — Associational (H2, n = 3,075, 222 enterers). Among respondents with non-co-resident children, within-person improvements in contact satisfaction were associated with lower loneliness change in the expected direction (β = −6.2 pp, p = 0.325 binary; β = 0.004, p = 0.990 continuous). While not statistically significant, the direction is consistent across specifications. The entry effect remains stable at 16.7 pp across Tier 1 and Tier 2, suggesting contact patterns do not fully mediate the entry effect. Reverse causality cannot be ruled out.

Tier 3 — Exploratory (H3 + H4, n = 393–395, already-transnational only). Distance alone was not significantly associated with loneliness change (β = 0.009, p = 0.726). However, the satisfaction × distance interaction was highly significant (β = −0.464, p < 0.001). Among households where satisfaction deteriorated, each additional 1,000 km increased loneliness by 0.481 points (p < 0.001). Among households where satisfaction improved, greater distance was associated with lower loneliness (−0.448 per 1,000 km, p < 0.001). These findings are preliminary given the small and structurally selected subsample.

15.2 Limitations

  • Time-varying unobserved confounding (Tier 1): Simultaneous health deterioration or economic shock could cause both migration and increasing loneliness, and cannot be ruled out without an instrument. IV using POEA municipality-level deployment data is a planned extension.

  • Reverse causality (Tier 2): Loneliness may reduce contact satisfaction rather than satisfaction buffering loneliness. Panel mediation with lagged variables would address this with three or more waves.

  • Structural missingness (Tier 3): 92% of the panel is missing destination country codes in at least one wave. The distance subsample (n = 395) is not representative of broader transnational households and contains zero newly-entering households, limiting generalisation.

  • Statistical power: With 223 enterers in the complete-case Tier 1 sample, the study is powered to detect standardised effects of approximately 0.20. Smaller true effects would require a larger panel or additional waves.

  • Attrition: Wave 2 has substantially more missing values on the 3-item loneliness scale (NA = 2,567 vs 775 at Wave 1), likely reflecting proxy interviews for frailer respondents. If attrition is non-random with respect to loneliness, Tier 1–2 estimates may understate the loneliness burden in the most vulnerable subgroup.
